events.body: Running status against the dbt project. Project parses clean — 64 models, 198 tests, last refresh 05:00 CST.
Running search for 'CNP fraud', 'card not present', 'merchant tier', 'Texas', 'billing_state', 'fraud signal'. Three upstream models surface: stg_card_transactions, stg_fraud_signals, dim_merchant.

Running describe on each candidate. stg_card_transactions is transaction-grain with channel, amount, merchant_id, and transaction_date. stg_fraud_signals joins on transaction_id with confirmed_fraud boolean. dim_merchant has merchant_tier and billing_state — that is the Texas and tier-2 slice the fraud desk needs.
events.side_effect: tool:describe returned grain=transaction_id on stg_card_transactions and stg_fraud_signals · grain=merchant_id on dim_merchant

events.body: Running lineage. Join path is clean: card transactions and fraud signals join on transaction_id, merchant dim attaches via merchant_id. Gap is confirmed — no model aggregates CNP confirmed-fraud to (merchant_tier, region, transaction_date). That is exactly what gold.fct_cnp_fraud_by_merchant_tier_region_daily needs to be.
events.side_effect: tool:lineage validated join path · gap confirmed at daily aggregation grain

events.body: Spinning up an XS warehouse and running dbt_show on a 14-day slice filtered to channel = 'CNP' and billing_state = 'TX'. CNP fraud rate for tier-2 merchants on 2026-05-13 is 2.4x the 30-day average — the spike is real and the grain is viable.
events.side_effect: tool:warehouse start XS · tool:dbt_show 14-day CNP slice TX · tier-2 spike 2.4x confirmed

events.body: Authoring models/gold/fct_cnp_fraud_by_merchant_tier_region_daily.sql. Writing the config block first — table materialization, gold schema, tags fraud and ai_built.
Writing the header docblock so downstream readers know what this model is and who built it.
events.code_append:
-- gold.fct_cnp_fraud_by_merchant_tier_region_daily
-- CNP confirmed-fraud counts, amounts, and rates rolled up to
-- (merchant_tier, region, transaction_date)
-- Built live by dbt-wizard sub-agents in response to fraud-desk escalation
-- Sources: stg_card_transactions + stg_fraud_signals + dim_merchant

events.body: Anchoring the card transactions CTE to CNP channel only — that is the scope the fraud desk asked about.
events.code_append:
with cnp_txns as (
    select
        t.transaction_id,
        t.merchant_id,
        t.transaction_date,
        t.amount_usd
    from {{ ref('stg_card_transactions') }} t
    where t.channel = 'CNP'
),

events.body: Pulling in confirmed fraud signals and the merchant dimension — tier and billing state are both on dim_merchant.
events.code_append:
fraud as (
    select
        transaction_id,
        confirmed_fraud,
        fraud_type
    from {{ ref('stg_fraud_signals') }}
),

merchants as (
    select
        merchant_id,
        merchant_tier,
        billing_state as region
    from {{ ref('dim_merchant') }}
),

events.body: Final SELECT — one row per (merchant_tier, region, transaction_date). Fraud count, total transaction count, confirmed amount, and fraud rate. Ordered so the highest-rate tier-region-day combination surfaces first.
events.code_append:
joined as (
    select
        m.merchant_tier,
        m.region,
        t.transaction_date,
        f.confirmed_fraud,
        t.amount_usd
    from cnp_txns t
    join merchants m on m.merchant_id = t.merchant_id
    left join fraud f on f.transaction_id = t.transaction_id
)

select
    merchant_tier,
    region,
    transaction_date,
    count(*) as cnp_transaction_count,
    sum(case when confirmed_fraud = true then 1 else 0 end) as confirmed_fraud_count,
    sum(case when confirmed_fraud = true then amount_usd else 0 end) as confirmed_fraud_amount_usd,
    sum(case when confirmed_fraud = true then 1 else 0 end)
        / nullif(count(*), 0) as cnp_fraud_rate
from joined
group by 1, 2, 3
order by cnp_fraud_rate desc, transaction_date desc

events.body: Writing the YAML companion. Description first, then the schema contract — every column gets a type so downstream consumers can resolve it without ambiguity.
events.code_append:
version: 2

models:
  - name: fct_cnp_fraud_by_merchant_tier_region_daily
    description: |
      CNP confirmed-fraud counts, amounts, and rates aggregated to
      (merchant_tier, region, transaction_date). Built by dbt-wizard
      sub-agents on 2026-05-22 in response to the fraud-desk escalation
      on the Texas tier-2 CNP spike.
    config:
      contract:
        enforced: true
      tags: [fraud, ai_built]
      meta:
        owner: fraud_analytics
        built_by: dbt-wizard
        consumes: [stg_card_transactions, stg_fraud_signals, dim_merchant]

events.body: Adding column-level tests. Uniqueness on the composite key (merchant_tier, region, transaction_date). Not-null on fraud rate. Accepted values on merchant_tier so tier drift is caught immediately.
events.code_append:
columns:
      - name: merchant_tier
        data_type: varchar
        tests:
          - not_null
          - accepted_values:
              values: ['tier-1', 'tier-2', 'tier-3']
      - name: region
        data_type: varchar
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: transaction_date
        data_type: date
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: cnp_transaction_count
        data_type: number
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: confirmed_fraud_count
        data_type: number
      - name: confirmed_fraud_amount_usd
        data_type: number
      - name: cnp_fraud_rate
        data_type: number
        tests:
          - not_null

    tests:
      - dbt_utils.unique_combination_of_columns:
          combination_of_columns: [merchant_tier, region, transaction_date]

events.body: Running dbt_show on the compiled model against the same 14-day CNP slice Worker used. All column tests resolve clean. Schema contract enforces. The Texas tier-2 spike on 2026-05-13 is visible in the first row.
events.side_effect: tool:dbt_show compiled · tests:passed · contract:enforced · TX tier-2 spike confirmed

events.body: Materializing gold.fct_cnp_fraud_by_merchant_tier_region_daily to Iceberg on S3 via the Snowflake dbt adapter. Writing Parquet files to the gold S3 prefix. Registering with AWS Glue Data Catalog.

Running lineage one more time. Three upstreams attached. Downstream consumers — AML analysts, BI dashboards, Snowflake direct queries — can now resolve the new gold table on their next pass. The fraud desk's question has a production asset behind it.
events.side_effect: tool:lineage updated · downstream consumers notified · 64 models → 65 models
